title: System Saviour: A Modular Diagnostic Device for Accessible Tech Repair
summary: This project proposes System Saviour, a rugged, modular, offline-compatible diagnostic device addressing the critical gap in affordable tech repair tools across South Africa, where device replacement costs are prohibitive and e-waste reached 360,000 tonnes in 2024 with only 14% properly recycled. Grounded in Cradle-to-Cradle design principles, circular economy frameworks, and right-to-repair ethics, the research identifies that South African users in township and low-income contexts lack standardised diagnostic tools. System Saviour is a compact handheld unit built from recycled plastic with modular adapter ports, a 12-hour battery, thermal sensors, and a multilingual UI supporting isiZulu, English, and Sesotho, capable of producing a full hardware and software diagnostic report in under 30 seconds with real-time part pricing. Use case testing across an informal repair stall in Khayelitsha, a school ICT lab, and a corporate tech environment demonstrated significant cost savings and a 70% reduction in diagnosis time.
